data.title: Solana Treasury Firm Secures $25 Million in Private Funding
data.description: According to PANews, Solana Treasury firm SOL Strategies, listed on Nasdaq under the ticker FTEL, has announced a $25 million private placement financing. This funding was conducted in accordance with the Canadian Securities Administrators' exemption requirements for listed issuer financing. The company raised the funds through the sale of common shares and share purchase warrants. Canaccord Genuity Corp. acted as the lead agent and exclusive bookrunner for the transaction. The proceeds from this financing will be allocated towards general corporate purposes and digital asset investments, aiming to drive the growth of SOL Treasury.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Benson: It's unclear whether the U.S. government will shut down next week
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Benson said it is unclear whether the U.S. government will shut down next week. (Jinshi)

data.title: Nasdaq-listed Iveda announces the creation of a cryptocurrency treasury
data.description: Iveda, a Nasdaq-listed provider of cloud-based video AI search and monitoring technology solutions, announced the establishment of a cryptocurrency treasury strategy. Its board of directors has authorized the purchase of cryptocurrencies to achieve asset diversification, but the company has not yet disclosed the specific cryptocurrencies it intends to purchase. (Businesswire)

data.title: The EU wants to restart tariff discussions with US on aluminum and steel
data.description:
The European Union announced Wednesday it plans to revive discussions with the United States over reducing fees on steel and aluminum shipments, as multiple nations work to resolve ongoing trade tensions. EU trade commissioner Maros Sefcovic will sit down with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer during the coming days to discuss removing or cutting back […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/eu-restart-tariff-discussions-steel-usa/

data.title: US Treasury Secretary Benson: We need to at least return to a neutral interest rate
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Benson said we need to lower interest rates, at least to return to a neutral rate, and he also said he would wait and see how the AI ​​craze develops. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Benson: Powell should signal a 100-150 basis point rate cut
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Benson said he was surprised that Federal Reserve Chairman Powell had not yet hinted at an interest rate target. The revisions to the employment data suggest something is amiss. He added that with several Fed interviews scheduled next week, Powell should signal a 100-150 basis point rate cut.

data.title: Stablecoin startup Bastion raises $14.6 million in funding, led by Coinbase
data.description: Stablecoin startup Bastion announced that it has completed a $14.6 million financing round, led by Coinbase, with participation from the venture capital arm of Japanese tech giant Sony, the venture capital arm of South Korean mobile phone manufacturer Samsung, the cryptocurrency arm of venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz, and cryptocurrency venture capital firm Hashed. (Fortune)

data.title: Tether Appoints Benjamin Habbel as Chief Commercial Officer
data.description: According to the official announcement, Tether appointed Benjamin Habbel as Chief Business Officer. He co-founded Limestone Capital, a global private equity firm focusing on alternative assets and served as CEO. He will lead corporate development, finance, investment and portfolio expansion.

data.title: US Treasury Secretary Benson: The Federal Reserve's interest rates have been too high for too long and will enter an easing cycle
data.description: US Treasury Secretary Benson said that the Federal Reserve's interest rates have been too high for too long; we will enter an easing cycle. (Jinshi)

data.title: A certain address purchased 1.667 million ASTER tokens at $0.09, and currently has a profit of 26 times.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a certain address purchased 1.667 million ASTER tokens at $0.09 on the day ASTER launched, generating a 26-fold profit, or approximately $3.8 million. This address transferred 152,000 USDT to Aster on the day ASTER launched (September 17th) and withdrew 1.667 million ASTER tokens (worth $3.95 million) from Aster 15 minutes prior to the launch, transferring them to Gate Exchange. This means the address spent only $0.09 to purchase these ASTER tokens, resulting in a profit of $3.8 million.

data.title: Binance Futures will delist UXLINK USDT U-margined Perpetual Contracts
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance Futures will automatically liquidate the UXLINKUSDT U-margined perpetual contract at 17:00 (GMT+8) on September 26, 2025, and will delist the above-mentioned U-margined perpetual contract trading pair after the liquidation is completed.

data.title: Big Pi Ventures, a blockchain-focused venture capital firm, has completed its first round of fundraising of €130 million for its new fund.
data.description: Athens-based Big Pi Ventures has just closed the first round of €130 million for its newly established growth fund to support Greek startups in their scalability efforts. Big Pi Ventures focuses on startups with strong intellectual property in the fields of artificial intelligence, biotechnology, blockchain, and deep tech. In addition to providing funding, the firm actively supports portfolio companies and leverages its international network to drive their growth. A key requirement is that the companies maintain a substantial presence in Greece. (TFN)

data.title: Binance announces Aster trading volume bonus, but it’s unrelated to Binance Alpha Points system
data.description: Binance has officially announced an exclusive bonus for Aster Trading Volume Points. Starting September 24, 2025, at 20:00 (UTC+8), Aster Trading Volume Points earned through interactions on the Aster dApp via the Binance Keyless Wallet (app or browser extension) will receive an additional 10% bonus. Aster will settle user Aster Trading Volume Points every Monday, covering valid trading data from the previous week (Monday to Sunday). The settlement results will be based on Aster's official data. After settlement, the bonus points will be displayed on the Aster website. Aster Trading Volume Points are internal to the Aster dApp and are unrelated to the Binance Alpha Points system.

data.title: Jiuzi New Energy's board of directors approves up to $1 billion in crypto asset investments
data.description: Jiuzi New Energy, a US-listed company, announced that its board of directors has officially approved and adopted a crypto asset investment policy, authorizing the company to deploy up to $1 billion to purchase crypto assets to ensure manageable risk. The policy authorizes Jiuzi New Energy to allocate a portion of its cash reserves to select crypto assets within a prudent risk management framework. Initial investments will be limited to BTC, ETH, and BNB. Any future expansion into other assets will require reassessment and approval by the board's risk committee. (PR Newswire)

data.title: Charles Schwab CEO: I am indeed concerned about young investors interested in cryptocurrencies
data.description: The CEO of Charles Schwab expressed concern about young investors interested in cryptocurrencies. (Zhitong Finance) Schwab previously released the results of its Q3 2025 Trader Sentiment Survey, which showed a significant rebound in market optimism. Among traders, 43% were bullish on cryptocurrencies, 38% on Bitcoin options ETFs, and 32% on spot cryptocurrency ETFs, all higher than in Q2 2025.

data.title: Native Markets: Over $15 million USDH minted in the past 24 hours
data.description: Hyperliquid's stablecoin, USDH, has officially launched after Native Markets won the bid. Native Markets announced on the X platform that USDH is now available to all Hyperliquid users. The USDH/USDC spot order book is now open on HyperCore. Over $15 million in USDH has been minted in the past 24 hours.

data.title: DeFi Technologies' Valour launches 13 cryptocurrency ETPs including HYPE
data.description: Valour, a subsidiary of Nasdaq-listed DeFi Technologies, announced the launch of 13 cryptocurrency ETPs denominated in Swedish krona on the Swedish Spotlight Stock Market. These include PEPE (PEPE), Flare (FLR), Virtuals (VIRTUAL), Optimism (OP), Story (IP), Immutable (IMX), Quant (QNT), The Graph (GRT), Floki (FLOKI), Theta (THETA), Four (FORM), IOTA (IOTA), and Hyperliquid (HYPE), bringing the total number of its digital asset ETP products to 99. (Globenewswire)

data.title: K33: The derivatives market reflects defensiveness and remains cautious about the recent trend of BTC
data.description: K33 pointed out in its analysis report: Monday's plunge caused the evaporation of $285 million in long positions and triggered $1.6 billion in liquidations across the entire cryptocurrency market. This plunge was the fourth largest liquidation event for Bitcoin in 2025 and the highest single-day liquidation of digital assets since February. In addition, BTC's seven-day actual volatility fell to just 0.6% last week, the lowest level since August 2023. Once positions are overly tilted, sudden chaos may occur. K 33 remains cautious about the near-term outlook, and the broader derivatives market reflects the same defensiveness. Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) futures trading remains sluggish, with the September contract once falling below the spot price. (The Block)

data.title: Hyperliquid’s USDH stablecoin officially launched after winning the bid on Native Markets
data.description: Native Markets' USDH stablecoin has launched on the Hyperliquid platform, with the USDH/USDC trading pair now open. Initial trading volume indicates a cautious but active start to the market, reaching approximately $2.2 million as the market launches. USDH is the first dollar-pegged token issued through Hyperliquid's validator selection process. It is a stablecoin natively issued on the HyperEVM and can be traded across the Hyperliquid ecosystem. Native Markets won the bid earlier this month, beating proposals from several companies, including Paxos, Frax, and Agora. (The Block)
